name: Adicet Bio, Inc.
business: Adicet Bio, Inc. is a clinical stage biotechnology company discovering and developing allogeneic gamma delta T cell therapies for autoimmune diseases and cancer. Its lead product candidate, prula-cel, is a first-in-class allogeneic gamma delta T cell therapy expressing a CAR targeting CD20 for potential treatment of autoimmune diseases. The company is also advancing ADI-212, a next-generation gene-edited and armored clinical candidate targeting PSMA for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer.

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical stage biotechnology company discovering and developing allogeneic gamma delta T cell therapies for autoimmune diseases and cancer.

catalysts.event: FDA meeting to inform potential pivotal trial design
catalysts.sentence: We plan to meet with the FDA in the second quarter of 2026 to inform potential pivotal trial design.

catalysts.event: Initiate potential pivotal study in LN or LN and SLE patients
catalysts.sentence: Subject to regulatory clearance to proceed, we expect to initiate a potential pivotal study in LN or LN and SLE patients in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Clinical update in LN, SLE and SSc patients
catalysts.sentence: Phase 1 enrollment is ongoing and we expect to provide a clinical update for this trial in LN, SLE and SSc patients in the first half of 2026, with a plan to provide an additional update in the second half of 2026.
The next clinical update on this trial is expected in the second half of 2026.
We expect to submit a regulatory filing for ADI-212 for the treatment of metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) in the third quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: Initiate patient enrollment for ADI-212 trial
catalysts.sentence: Subject to regulatory clearance to proceed with a clinical trial, we expect to initiate patient enrollment in the fourth quarter of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Greater than 1.7 million patients with autoimmune diseases where CAR-T cell therapy has demonstrated clinical proof-of-concept
tamClaims.sentence: We believe the potential market opportunity for prula-cel in B cell mediated autoimmune diseases is substantial based on the prevalence in the U.S., EU5, China and Japan of greater than 1.7 million patients with autoimmune diseases where CAR-T cell therapy has demonstrated clinical proof-of-concept, including SLE (which includes LN), SSc, IIM and SPS.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 75,000 patients with second or third line advanced mCRPC
tamClaims.sentence: We believe the potential market opportunity for ADI-212 in mCRPC is significant based on the prevalence in the U.S., EU5, China and Japan of approximately 75,000 patients with second or third line advanced disease.
